Title: $0/Month, 5 AI Apps, All on Gemini: Here's Exactly What the Free Tier Gives You (and What Breaks First)
URL: https://dev.to/tahosin/0month-5-ai-apps-all-on-gemini-heres-exactly-what-the-free-tier-gives-you-and-what-breaks-58fl
Published: 2026-04-22T22:15:57.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
Google's Gemini free tier allows users to ship five AI products, all live on the company's free tier. However, these products still cost $0/month. The architecture of these five apps, including Hocks AI, requires no backend server, database, paid auth, no paid CDN, and uses Firebase. The exception is the exception to this, using Firebase for chat history, image uploads, and a user system. The free Gemini quota changes without warning, causing live apps to call the wrong model and hit 429s within an hour of the change. The only cost for each free tier is a $5/month GCP e2-micro VM running a separate project. The author also highlights the challenges of the free tier's architecture and its limitations.

---

Title: Google AI Studio Pricing 2026: Free Tier, API Costs & Plans
URL: https://www.nocode.mba/articles/google-ai-studio-pricing
Published: 2026-04-06T00:00:00.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
Google AI Studio Pricing 2026: Free Tier
[...]
Yes — Google AI Studio is completely free to use. There are no subscriptions, seat-based fees, or paid tiers. You can prototype prompts, test Gemini models, and even build AI agents at zero cost. Charges only begin when you move to the Gemini Developer API for production use, where token-based pricing starts at $0.10 per million input tokens (Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite).
[...]
| Tier | Cost | Best For | Billing |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Google AI Studio (Interface) | 🏆 Free | Prototyping, prompt testing, building agents | No billing required |
| Gemini Developer API (Free Tier) | Free (rate-limited) | Small projects, testing | No billing required |
| Gemini Developer API (Paid Tier) | Pay-as-you-go | Production apps, higher limits | Per-token pricing |
| Vertex AI (Enterprise) | Custom / Pay-as-you-go | Enterprise, SLAs, compliance | Token + infrastructure costs |
[...]
The key insight: Google AI Studio is always free. It's the Gemini API underneath that has paid tiers — and even the API has a generous free tier for most models.
[...]
within the Studio
[...]
itself. You can sign in with any Google account and start building immediately.
[...]
When you generate an API key from AI Studio, you automatically get access to the free tier of the Gemini Developer API. This includes free input and output tokens for most models — the catch is lower rate limits compared to the paid tier.
[...]
Yes, Google AI Studio as a browser-based interface is completely free. There are no subscriptions, seat fees, or hidden costs. You can test prompts, build prototypes, and create AI agents without paying anything. Costs only arise when you use the Gemini API for production deployments.
[...]
There's no "trial" because AI Studio is permanently free. You can use it indefinitely with a Google account. The Gemini API also has a permanent free tier — it's not a time-limited trial but a rate-limited free plan that never expires.

Title: What Are Google AI Studio's Free Tier Limits and Rate Limits in 2026 | BSWEN
URL: https://docs.bswen.com/blog/2026-03-23-google-ai-studio-free-tier-limits
Published: 2026-03-23T00:00:00.000Z
Author: Cowrie   Dev @ Bswen
Highlights:
Google AI Studio enforces three main types of rate limits on the free tier:
[...]
RPM (Requests Per Minute) - How many API calls you can make in a 60-second window. This is the most common limit you’ll hit during development when testing multiple calls in quick succession.
[...]
TPM (Tokens Per Minute) - The total number of input and output tokens processed per minute. This matters when you’re sending large prompts or getting long responses. A single request with a 50,000-token prompt could consume most of your TPM budget.
[...]
RPD (Requests Per Day) - The total number of requests allowed in a 24-hour period. Once you hit this, you’re done until the quota resets.
[...]
```
| Model           | RPM  | TPM     | RPD   ||-----------------|------|---------|-------|| Gemini 1.5 Flash| 15   | 1M      | 1,500 || Gemini 1.5 Pro  | 2    | 32K     | 50    || Gemini 2.0 Flash | 15  | 1M      | 1,500 || Gemini 2.0 Pro   | 2    | 32K     | 50    |
```
[...]
Google AI Studio’s free tier offers generous limits for prototyping and personal projects. Flash models get approximately 15 RPM, 1M TPM, and 1,500 RPD. Pro models are more restricted at around 2 RPM and 32K TPM.
[...]
The key to using these limits effectively is proactive monitoring and graceful handling. Don’t wait for 429 errors to crash your app. Track your usage, implement retries with exponential backoff, and fail gracefully when limits are reached.
[...]
Most importantly, stay informed. Community reports suggest free tier limits may change, and relying solely on the free tier for production applications carries risk. Always verify current limits on the official Google AI documentation.

---

Title: How much is the Google AI Studio free quota? 3 solutions after reaching the limit - Apiyi.com Blog
URL: https://help.apiyi.com/en/google-ai-studio-free-quota-limits-solution-en.html
Published: 2026-01-24T04:28:21.000Z
Author: APIYI - Stable and affordable AI API
Highlights:
On December 7, 2025, Google AI Studio's free tier took a major hit, with daily quotas for some models slashed by 50-80%. Here's the latest breakdown of the free limits as of early 2026:
[...]
| Model | RPM (Requests Per Minute) | TPM (Tokens Per Minute) | RPD (Requests Per Day) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | 5 | 250,000 | 100 |
| Gemini 2.5 Flash | 10 | 250,000 | 250 |
| Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite | 15 | 250,000 | 1,000 |
| Gemini 3 Pro Series | 5-10 | 100,000 | 50-100 |
| Nano Banana Pro | 2-5 | – | Extremely Low |
[...]

Requests Per Minute): Number of requests per minute; the free tier only allows 5-15.
[...]

4. RPD (Requests Per Day): Total requests per day. This is the limit people hit
[...]

[...]
, specifically for image models.
[...]
- Quotas are calculated per project, not per API key.
- RPD resets at midnight Pacific Time (which is 4:00 PM Beijing Time).
- RPM and TPM use a rolling 60-second window; they don't reset at fixed intervals.
- Following the December 2025 update, these limits are being enforced much more strictly.
[...]

Recommendation: If you frequently hit 429 errors or need to generate images with Nano Banana Pro at scale, we recommend using APIYI (apiyi.com). It's 80% cheaper than official prices, has no RPD limits, and supports local payment methods like Alipay and WeChat.
[...]
- Wait for the reset (best for light usage).
- Upgrade to a paid plan (requires an international credit card, and quota increases are limited).
- APIYI (apiyi.com) (Recommended: 20% of the price, no limits).
[...]
For developers who need to use the Gemini API heavily—especially for Nano Banana Pro image generation—we recommend calling it via APIYI (apiyi.com). 4K images cost only $0.05 each (compared to $0.24 officially), there are no RPD limits, and they support Alipay/WeChat along with Chinese technical support.

Author: Your Fix Expert
Highlights:
How To Deploy Your Google AI Studio App For Free (2026) (Best Method) In this video, you’ll learn how to deploy your Google AI Studio app for free using the best and easiest method in 2026. This beginner-friendly tutorial shows step by step how to deploy Google AI Studio app without paying, even if you’re new to Google AI tools.
[...]
We’ll cover how to deploy Google AI Studio app for free, the correct setup process, and tips to manage your Google AI Studio project deployment efficiently. You’ll also learn how to host your Google AI app, use Google AI Studio features, and make your app accessible online.

[...]

[...]
In today's video, I'm going to teach you how to deploy your Google AI Studio app for free. By the end, you'll have a working app deployed from Google AI Studio without spending a scent as long as you stay within the free usage limits. Make sure you're signed into a Google account before you begin so everything saves properly. First, open your browser and go to a studio.google.com.
[...]
Before you publish, double check that the app doesn't expose private information and that it responds safely and reliably to edge cases. You'll be asked to select a cloud project. If you already have a Google Cloud project with the necessary permissions, choose it now. Staying within the free quota is the key to keeping costs at zero. So, pick a project you manage and monitor.
[...]
If prompted, allow any required APIs to be enabled. The deployment flow can handle that for you. If you don't have a project yet, create one. Give it a clear, memorable name and confirm your settings. Keep everything under the default free free limits and you're good to go. After creation, select that project to proceed. Finish the deployment and wait a moment while it publishes. You'll receive a live URL you can bookmark and share. Test the live app to confirm it matches your preview.
